summary: Do not ship: the new endpoint misses the accepted request/response contract, allows prompt-path escape via unsanitized mode names, and collapses real config/router failures into misleading success/404 paths.

findings.title: `/api/orchestrator/place` does not implement the accepted API contract
findings.recommendation: Parse and validate `cwd` and `worktreeId`, honor `message`/sticky semantics as required, and return the full accepted payload (`soul`, `promptPath`, `model`, `effort`, `sessionId`, `mode`, `role`). Add an endpoint-level test for the response shape and `channel=dev-env` defaulting to `joe`.

findings.title: Mode names from `modes.json` are written directly into a filesystem path
findings.recommendation: Treat mode ids as untrusted input from config: reject path separators / `..`, or map modes to sanitized filenames before joining with `outDir`. Prefer a confinement check on the final resolved path before writing.

findings.title: Configuration and routing failures are misclassified or silently downgraded
findings.recommendation: Differentiate missing `modes.json` from malformed/invalid config, and fail the request when routing config cannot produce the promised `model`/`effort` rather than returning `200` with null fallbacks. Surface these as explicit 4xx/5xx errors and cover them in tests.
